understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the KA59 puzzle game by solving each level within its step budget, where a level is solved only when every goal tile marked '+' is simultaneously occupied by a selectable piece (and if there are multiple goals, all must be covered at once).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) moved the currently selected piece on a grid expressed in pixel coordinates (3 pixels per cell), with x increasing to the right and y increasing downward. Walls generally blocked movement, while some objects were pushable blocks that could be pushed if adjacent in the movement direction (causing the block to shift along with the piece if space allowed). Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with a target_position matching an on-screen selectable’s pixel coordinate switched control to that piece. The movement.*_blocked flags were only hints indicating adjacency and did not guarantee success or failure of a move.
